hints.noun: 'Vae' is not a noun — it is an exclamation expressing grief or warning
hints.verb: 'Vae' is not a verb — it is an interjection meaning 'woe!'
hints.adverb: 'Vae' is not an adverb — it is an interjection (exclamation)

hints.nominative: Nominative 2nd person plural is 'vos' — 'vobis' is dative/ablative
hints.accusative: Accusative 2nd person plural is 'vos' — 'vobis' is dative here (woe TO you)
hints.ablative: 'vobis' can be ablative, but after 'vae' it is dative (woe to you)

hints.nominative: Nominative plural would be 'divites' — 'divitibus' is dative/ablative plural
hints.accusative: Accusative plural would be 'divites' — 'divitibus' is dative, in apposition to 'vobis'
hints.ablative: 'divitibus' could be ablative, but here it is dative, agreeing with 'vobis'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'divitis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'divitis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -us — 'divitis' ends in -is

steps.prompt: This phrase has no finite verb. What verb is implied?

hints.venit (comes): 'Vae' is an exclamation — the implied verb is 'est' or 'sit' (woe IS to you)
hints.habet (has): 'Vae' takes a dative — the implied copula is 'est', not 'habet'
hints.dat (gives): 'Vae' is an interjection with dative — the implied verb is 'est/sit'

translation: Woe to you who are rich
